summary: LYRO end-to-end verification: 28/28 backend pytest cases green (auth, syllabus, batches, students, tests, marks, dashboard analytics, student profile, AI endpoints — Claude Sonnet 4.5 via emergentintegrations, cascading deletes, data isolation). Frontend Playwright: login → batch creation → students → tests → dashboard at-risk row → StudentProfile chart + history + all 3 AI generations (summary, parent-message, monthly-report) render correctly. Mobile 375px marks page renders. Only issues noted are: (a) intermittent Playwright/React race when typing into MarksEntry <input type=number> fields (one of 3 fills sometimes loses value); (b) a11y/HTML warning (span inside <option>). No functional bugs.

ui_bugs.issue: React hydration warning: <span> cannot be a child of <option>. Emergent's runtime injects a display:contents <span> wrapper inside every <option> element (visible in console for 'Same as batch ({course})' option and other dynamic options). Not user-visible but pollutes console.

action_items: OPTIONAL: The Playwright fills into MarksEntry score-input-<id> occasionally lose a value (state race with React controlled inputs). Real users won't hit this; it only surfaced with programmatic .fill()/.type(). If you want it 100% robust for future automation, consider dispatching a manual 'input' event via evaluate() or using .press_sequentially. No product change required — human input works fine.

OPTIONAL: Suppress or remove <span> wrappers injected inside <option> children (source of hydration warning). Emergent's dev-mode source annotator wraps dynamic children in a <span style='display:contents'>. Not blocking.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py at 679 lines is at the soft ceiling; consider splitting routers (auth/syllabus/batches/students/tests/marks/analytics/ai) into /app/backend/routers/*.py as feature-set grows.
load_dotenv() is called before imports that read env — good ordering (auth_utils reads JWT_SECRET at import). Kept correct.
delete_batch cascades correctly (marks → tests → students → batch). Verified.

_batch_rank recomputes across ALL batch marks on every profile/summary/parent-message/monthly-report call — for a batch with many students/tests this can be O(N*M) per call. If batch size grows, consider caching per-batch ranking on dashboard call and reusing.
Score validation only clamps 0..max_marks; no NaN guard on the server side because Pydantic float parsing rejects non-numeric strings. Good enough for v1.
generate_* AI endpoints have no timeout wrapper around chat.send_message; a slow Anthropic call will hold the request open. Consider asyncio.wait_for(..., timeout=45) with graceful 504 fallback.
No rate-limiting on AI endpoints — a malicious owner could burn the Emergent LLM budget. Not v1-critical but worth noting.
monthly-report endpoint uses `month: Optional[str] = None` as a query param — matches the frontend which passes ?month=YYYY-MM. Good.

Frontend correctly reads REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL, all interactive elements have data-testid. StudentProfile.jsx uses conditional rendering for copy/whatsapp buttons (only after content generated) — expected behavior.
test_credentials: owner@lyro.demo / demo1234 (seeded by backend startup — verified working)
seed_data_creation: Created two TEST_ batches via API to validate at-risk analytics + AI generation for StudentProfile UI. Batch b1ace071-094f-4f09-a1c5-b64d7ba271f8 with student 'Aarav Kumar' (43af02d0-ad3f-4fe6-804c-24e1d71e9bc5) at 80%→40% shows drop_percent=40 in at-risk list. Additional TEST_ batches created & cleaned up by pytest fixtures. Some TEST_ batches remain (from UI runs) and can be safely deleted manually.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py runs cleanly under 30s (uses REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL env). AI endpoint tests take ~10-15s combined. Demo owner is idempotently seeded on startup. Data-testids across all pages match the spec. If retesting MarksEntry via Playwright, avoid rapid page.fill on consecutive score-input-* fields — use page.type with delay=50 + press Tab, or seed marks via POST /api/tests/{id}/marks then only test the read path.
rca_of_issues: Playwright rapid .fill() on React controlled <input type=number> in MarksEntry sometimes loses one of several values because the controlled `value` prop gets re-rendered from stale state between synthetic input events. Reproduction: within 100ms window, do 3 page.fill on 3 different score-input-*; observe that occasionally only 1-2 values persist after Save. Mitigation for automated tests: page.type with per-keystroke delay + Tab press to force onBlur/settle. Not observed with human input.
